texto: I have a message for your daughter,” said Cale. “I am bound to her with cables that not even God can break. One day, if there is a soft breeze on her cheek, it may be my breath; one night, if the cool wind plays with her hair, it may be my shadow passing by.” And with this terrible threat he faced forward and the procession started once more. In less than a minute they were gone. In her shady room Arbell Swan-Neck stood white and cold as alabaster.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Hoffman sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “I have a message for your daughter,” said Cale. “I am bound to her with cables that not even God …” articula sucesso, alabaster, arbell em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: I have a message for your daughter,” said Cale. “I am bound to her with cables that not even God can break. One day, if there is a soft breeze on her cheek, it may be my breath; one night, if the cool wind plays with her hair, it may be my shadow passing by.” And with this terrible threat he faced forward and the procession started once more. In less than a minute they were gone. In her shady room Arbell Swan-Neck stood white and cold as alabaster.

texto: As it happens I don't think it was God- the woman who saved me, she doesn't look like she's had much to do with angels, Perhaps the Devil was behind me all the time.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Paul Hoffman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “As it happens I don't think it was God- the woman who saved me, she doesn't look like she's had m…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, salvation, happens pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
